page: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 64 vCPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and utilizes a dedicated CPU allocation. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage.

A key differentiator for the m6in.16xlarge is its high-speed networking capability, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, Passmark CPU scores, and software compilation with Clang. It also demonstrates strong performance in cryptographic operations, database operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6in.16xlarge offers a high-density resource profile suitable for enterprise workloads requiring high network throughput without the need for local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, making it dependent on EBS performance for disk I/O. This server type is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, while single-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. This configuration represents a balanced resource profile for network-intensive applications, though workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O must rely on external storage. It is highly suited for enterprise web serving, database management, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 100 Gbps networking for demanding multi-core enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations.
Dedicated CPU allocation running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
